profile_meta_description: Sightwise GmbH develops scalable AI-powered inspection solutions for industrial quality assurance, addressing key limitations of traditional machine vision. By leveraging synthetic data, the company fully automates the AI development lifecycle, significantly reducing time and cost. A key advantage is the ability to generate artificial NOK (defect) data, which is typically rare or difficult to capture in real production environments. This enables highly robust models for defect detection, even in complex or edge-case scenarios. Customers benefit from rapid deployment, high detection accuracy, and seamless scalability across production lines. The result is clear business impact through reduced scrap rates, improved process efficiency, and accelerated time-to-value.

profile_meta_description: Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Berlin, GESTALT Automation GmbH is an industrial integrator for AI-powered Smart Inspection Systems. The company develops and industrializes inline and end-of-line inspection solutions for real-time industrial quality assurance.  A key technological focus lies in the combination of hyperspectral imaging and artificial intelligence. This enables the detection of chemical compositions, contaminations, and hidden defects that cannot be identified using conventional machine vision systems – “See the Unseen.”  GESTALT follows a hybrid AI approach combining rule-based methods, anomaly detection, and deep learning, while integrating multiple sensor technologies into a unified industrial inspection architecture.  Its solutions are deployed across industries including automotive, aerospace, pharmaceuticals, food, energy, and electronics.

profile_meta_description: Sightwise GmbH is at the forefront of transforming industrial AI-based image processing by using synthetic data for AI development, a breakthrough that addresses the limitations of traditional image processing methods. Conventional approaches often struggle with complex and dynamic conditions, requiring significant time and resources for data collection and annotation. Sightwise overcomes these challenges by generating synthetic datasets from 3D objects within simulation environments, automatically producing the ideal image-label pairs necessary for developing robust inspection algorithms. This approach not only reduces development time and costs but also enhances data protection, making it a highly scalable and efficient solution for manufacturing companies of many sectors.  Visit us at our booth to connect and learn more about the use of synthetic data in industrial image processing!

Visit us at our booth to connect and learn more about the use of synthetic data in industrial image processing!

profile_meta_description: QualityStorm is an AI-driven platform for industrial visual inspection and quality traceability. We help manufacturers replace paper and spreadsheets with digital inspection plans executed on the shopfloor using fixed cameras or mobile devices. In the Training module, teams upload images, label defects/objects, build datasets, and train/validate multiple AI models per product or station. In the Execution module, operators select an inspection plan, capture images, and receive real-time pass/fail decisions with on-image markings. All results are stored with photos, timestamps, and dashboards for audits, CAPA, and continuous improvement. Typical outcomes: fewer defects and rework, faster inspections, standardized routines, and full traceability across plants.

profile_meta_description: mechIC develops and distributes sensors and system solutions for monitoring strain, force, and overload, whereby the sensors do not require any power to measure, store, and evaluate data. The first product is a strain sensor that detects mechanical overloads and serves as an “error memory.” Three variants are to be launched on the market: a real-time strain sensor, a semi-active overload sensor, and a passive sensor with integrated memory. The business model is based on the development and distribution of standardized sensor chips and system solutions. In addition to hardware, mechIC offers communication interfaces (e.g., LoRaWAN, Bluetooth, NFC) as well as basic software and an app for reading the sensors. Manufacturing is outsourced to semiconductor foundries, while development, calibration, firmware, and testing are handled by mechIC.

profile_meta_description: On September 1st, 1946 the Master machine builder Ludwig Boschert founded our company. Very soon the small company was known for its good quality workmanship and it grew quickly. Shortly thereafter the young company was taken over by the Kunz family. Since then, contacts and friendships have been built up all over the world. As a result, our plant has grown constantly, changing from a small workshop to a large industrial production facility. Boschert punching machines and notchers are known in sheet metal shops worldwide as machines that are built to last. In the field of safety chucks, as well, we have proven our forward-thinking ways with the many international patents we have been awarded for innovative new products. Boschert is a company with a bright future. By staying true to our heritage of innovative products we will continue the success of our past. As satisfied customers have been our incentive in the past, they remain our responsibility for the future.

profile_meta_description: Haply Robotics designs and manufactures high fidelity haptic controllers for robotics, simulation, and human machine interaction. profile_meta_description: NanoSen develops next-generation force sensor technologies based on PNC materials in combination with our proprietary force sensing impedance (FSI) technology. Our materials form the basis for highly sensitive force and tactile sensors that are characterized by high signal stability, reliability, and mechanical robustness. By evaluating the frequency-dependent electrical behavior under load, force sensing impedance technology enables precise measurement of force and touch in real time over a wide measuring range.  Our solutions are used in medical technology, automation, and intelligent systems. NanoSen's goal is to provide innovative and scalable force sensor solutions that can be flexibly integrated into existing systems and enable new applications. We supply both standardized products and customer-specific developments – from the selection of suitable components to implementation in prototypes.

Zu unseren Produkten gehören u.a. Schneidringverschraubungen nach EN ISO 8434-1 (DIN 2353), Gewindefittings und Flansche. Kennzeichnung u.a. auf den Produkten ist die Kennzeichnung sf71. Die Schneidringverschraubung, die Klemmringverschraubung und spezielle Kugelhähne werden in eigenen europäischen Fertigungsstätten hergestellt. Schneidringverschraubungen sind hier der Klassiker im Angebot, natürlich auch im eShop erhältlich. Die Edelstahl u2-Lok Klemm-Keilring Rohrverschraubungen finden im Hochdruck- und Vakuumanwendungen ihren Einsatz, wo höchste Anforderungen an Material und Dichtheit bestehen. Sie eignet sich für flüssige und gasförmige Medien. Auch für Anwendungen mit gasförmigem Wasserstoff geeignet und nach EC79 zertifiziert (H2-Lok).

company_name: Fraunhofer-Institut für Mikrotechnik und Mikrosysteme IMM
profile_meta_description: Fraunhofer IMM is part of the Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft e.V. We concern with the mobile and decentralized provision and storage of electrical energy with a view to sustainable energy sources. We build on the use of our proven microstructured plate heat exchangers, a portfolio of highly active, long-term stable and robust catalysts, and established manufacturing technologies.  Our development work covers the entire technology chain from system design, process simulation, catalyst development, stand-time testing, reactor design, development of cost-effective manufacturing technologies, system control, system integration to system testing.  We develop individual components and complete reformer systems for hydrogen production for all types of fuel cells from conventional and renewable fuels. We conduct research in exhaust gas purification, power-to-gas, methanation, heat/cooling management and biofuel synthesis and the use of ammonia and methanol as hydrogen carrier.

profile_meta_description: ELB develops advanced surface solutions that extend the performance and service life of lightweight metal components. Our technologies enhance corrosion resistance, wear protection, and functional reliability of aluminum, magnesium, and titanium parts, even under demanding mechanical, thermal, or chemical conditions.  Within the CERANOD® framework, ELB modifies the surface itself rather than applying conventional coatings. This creates tightly bonded, ceramic-functional zones integral to the base material, ensuring durability while preserving full recyclability.  All surface solutions are PFAS-, chromium-, and VOC-free and support environmentally compliant, resource-efficient lightweight design. Depending on application requirements, ELB provides functional, protective, or aesthetic surface solutions. From application-oriented consulting and prototyping to scalable, series-ready process design, we create measurable added value for high-performance lightweight components.

DEVELOPERS WHO UNDERSTAND BOTH WORLDS.  ForestHub was founded by developers and engineers with deep experience in embedded systems, software architecture, and machine learning. company_name: Fraunhofer-Institut für Experimentelles Software Engineering IESE
profile_meta_description: The Fraunhofer ITWM uses computer simulations to support companies in the design/optimization of products/processes. Human models for the digital factory The digital human model IPS-IMMA is used in variety of industrial applications to design workstations under ergonomic aspects. It allows to analyze reachability, visibility and assemblability on digital prototypes while considering the human variance. Digital environmental data for the development/testing of assistance systems The software package VMC Road-and-Scene Generator enables the virtual development and proving of assistance systems and autonomous driving functions (Level 1-5) based on real environmental data. The institute's own laser scanner REDAR measures highly accurate georeferenced 3D point clouds of the environment. From those data, all relevant objects are automatically identified, classified and attributed for the purpose of sensor simulation.

The institutes of the Fraunhofer Group for Materials and Components develop sustainable innovations for mobility, mechanical and plant engineering, environmental and energy technology, construction, health and safety. Ours scientists’ materials expertise spans the entire industrial value chain, from fundamental research to materials-based system solutions.  The Group covers metallic, inorganic non-metallic, polymeric and renewable raw material-based materials. Hybrid materials and composites are especially important, for instance in lightweight construction. Particular focus is placed on resource efficiency and circularity.  Experimental work in laboratories, technical centers and pilot plants is complemented by numerical simulation and modeling. Digital representations of materials across their life cycle and all scales, from molecules to complex systems, are a high priority. Artificial intelligence methods are used more and more frequently.
website_home_meta_description: Die Institute des Fraunhofer-Verbunds Materials schaffen nachhaltige Innovationen in den Bereichen Mobilität, Maschinen- und Anlagenbau, Energie & Umwelt, Bauen, Gesundheit und Sicherheit. Dazu setzen die WissenschaftlerInnen ihre Materialkompetenz entlang industrieller Wertschöpfungsketten ein, von materialwissenschaftlichen Grundlagen bis hin zu werkstoffbasierten Systemlösungen. Stofflich deckt der Fraunhofer-Verbund Materials den gesamten Bereich der metallischen, anorganisch-nichtmetallischen, polymeren und aus nachwachsenden Rohstoffen erzeugten Werkstoffe ab. Große Bedeutung haben hybride Materialien und Verbundwerkstoffe, etwa für Leichtbauanwendungen. Dabei erhalten Aspekte der Ressourceneffizienz und Kreislauffähigkeit besondere Beachtung. Experimentelle Arbeiten in Labors, Technika und Pilotanlagen werden von Verfahren der numerischen Simulation und Modellierung begleitet. Die digitale Abbildung von Werkstoffen über ihren gesamten Lebenszyklus und in allen Skalen, vom Molekül über das Bauteil bis hin zum komplexen System und zur Prozesssimulation, hat für die Verbundinstitute einen hohen Stellenwert. Zunehmend werden Methoden der KI eingesetzt.

profile_meta_description: GfM Gesellschaft für Maschinendiagnose mbH specializes in the diagnosis of industrial gearboxes with rolling bearings. Using vibration analysis, it is possible to detect even the slightest irregularities in gearboxes and rolling bearings at an early stage. These irregularities can include damage to rolling bearings, gears, and shafts, as well as imbalance and misalignment. In addition, the company offers monitoring of extremely slow-running drive components such as converter bearings in steel mills. Crane runway monitoring, drum coupling monitoring, foundation monitoring, torque measurement, and torsional vibration analysis round out the product range. GfM offers both services and mobile systems as well as online condition monitoring systems that the operator can manage entirely on their own if desired. Seminars are also offered.

profile_meta_description: de Jonge GmbH develops energy-efficient embedded AI solutions for industrial systems. The goal is to make AI available exactly where data is generated: inside machines, sensors, and existing installations.  At the core is the inline approach. With EdgeBrain, a plug-and-play AI module, artificial intelligence can be integrated between sensor and host without cloud dependency and without interfering with existing IT/OT architectures. This enables inspection, monitoring, and decision-making processes to be executed deterministically, with low latency and high energy efficiency directly at the system level—from quality inspection to sensor fusion and condition monitoring.  Customers benefit from AI that delivers high performance at low power consumption, integrates seamlessly into real production environments, and whose decisions remain transparent and explainable. Optionally, EdgeBrain supports bidirectional, post-quantum-secure encryption for highly secure local or hybrid deployments.

AUTFORCE Automations GmbH is an industrial automation specialist with a strong focus on testing systems and industrial software. For more than 25 years, AUTFORCE has been delivering customized solutions for the manufacturing industry, with deep process expertise across the entire shopfloor. This close connection to production forms the basis for practical and reliable digitalization solutions.  Based on this experience, autfactory was developed – the software for optimizing production. autfactory creates shopfloor transparency by jointly capturing and analyzing machine performance and manual work steps. Core features include machine performance tracking (OEE) and AI-supported root cause analysis. Operator inputs and process knowledge are digitized and linked with machine data, even in heterogeneous brownfield environments. autfactory is web-based, easy to integrate, and supports production managers in sustainably increasing output.

profile_meta_description: Binabik.ai — The Agentic Operating System for Physical AI.  Binabik is building the world’s first agentic operating system for humanoid and robotic platforms — a modular "brain" that enables robots to learn faster from less data, comprehend complex tasks, and operate reliably in real-world, dynamic environments.  While others rely on massive datasets or rigid automation, Binabik proves its strength in AI-based learning within Small Data realities — thereby mastering dynamic interactions in high-mix industrial and professional scenarios. Our OS breaks down processes into reusable sub-steps, achieving up to 85% greater learning efficiency, real-time adaptation, and hardware independence.  Binabik.ai transforms standard robots into intelligent, autonomous collaborators — accelerating deployment, lowering costs, and paving the way for the next generation of Physical AI.

company_name: Fraunhofer-Institut für Fertigungstechnik und Angewandte Materialforschung IFAM
profile_meta_description: Vibrations in machines, tools, and equipment cause major issues across industries: reduced machining quality and accuracy, increased noise and emissions, accelerated wear, and impacts on comfort and safety. These effects are intensified by trends such as lightweight design, higher process speeds, increased power density, and rising demands for energy efficiency and acoustic performance.  Fraunhofer IFAM Dresden addresses these challenges with customized particle damping concepts. Powder-filled cavities are integrated into existing structures without compromising lightweight benefits. The solution is maintenance-free, lightweight, and highly effective.  The principle: particles move within cavities, interact through friction, and convert vibration energy into heat. Precise tuning of the powder formulation is key.  Our expertise lies in system analysis, simulation, and optimization for maximum damping performance in your application.

profile_meta_description: RIVA Power Systems develops high-temperature fuel cells for off-grid applications that supply CO₂-neutral electricity and heat. The aim is to replace diesel generators with flexible, efficient, and scalable solutions.  The development work included prototypes of stationary methanol fuel cells for micro combined heat and power generation in order to test combined electricity and heat generation in various performance classes. Building on this, the focus is now on containerized mobile systems and modular power generators.  Scaling includes more powerful modules, series production of proprietary membrane electrode assemblies on a pilot plant scale, and larger production capacities. The systems are intended for industry, charging infrastructure, emergency power, and decentralized energy supply, and will enable mobile fast-charging units or integration into decentralized energy centers in the future.

company_name: DTU Danmarks Tekniske Universitet Energy Conversion and Storage
profile_meta_description: In a sustainable energy system, a large part of the energy will be supplied by fluctuating sources such as solar and wind power. This makes it critically important to be able to convert and store the energy as needed. At DTU Energy we work on fuel cells, electrolysis, solar cells, batteries, magnetic refrigeration and other sustainable energy technologies. Extensive research activities in the area of fuel cells and electrolysis cover solid oxide cells (SOFC & SOEC), high temperature PEM, and alkaline electrolysis. DTU Energy provides access to advanced testing and demonstration of components and systems, training and consultancy to the industry within the fuel cell and hydrogen technologies through its FCH Test Center.

This includes the installation and provision of the necessary measuring equipment as well as an evaluation of the measurement results.

profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Deutsches Forschungszentrum DFKI
profile_meta_description: Human Centric AI DFKI has been researching on Human Centric AI for over 35 years and is oriented towards social relevance and scientific excellence in the key future-oriented research and application areas of artificial intelligence. The German Research Center for Artificial Intelligence (DFKI) was founded in 1988 as a non-profit public-private partnership. It has locations in Kaiserslautern, Saarbrücken, Bremen, Lower Saxony, laboratories in Berlin, Darmstadt and Lübeck as well as a branch office in Trier. In 29 research areas, ten competence centers and eight living labs, product functions, prototypes and patentable solutions in the field of information technology are developed on the basis of application-oriented basic research. In addition to the federal states of Rhineland-Palatinate, Saarland and Bremen, numerous well-known German and international high-tech companies from a wide range of industries are represented on the DFKI Supervisory Board.

EMQ provides powerful MQTT infrastructure for the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). With EMQX as a unified edge-to-cloud platform, manufacturers can capture, process, and seamlessly stream real-time data from devices and factory systems to cloud, analytics, and AI applications. As the central data backbone between OT and IT, EMQX consolidates shop-floor data into a Unified Namespace, laying the foundation for data transparency, automation, and AI-enabled operations. EMQX supports more than 100 industrial and IoT protocols, and over 50 integrations with other systems and applications such as Snowflake and Kafka. More than 1,000 customers worldwide in industries such as manufacturing, automotive, energy, telecommunications, and robotics rely on the EMQX Platform. Our European offices are in Frankfurt and Stockholm. Visit us for a live demo of real-time fault detection, automated inspection, and AI-assisted root cause analysis.

profile_meta_description: With FabOS, 24 project partners from science and industry are developing an operating system for production that enables the uniform integration of IT and OT. Following the principles of well-known operating systems to link hardware with apps and programmes, FabOS is to become a platform for machines, infrastructure and AI services. To enable technological sovereignty for AI applications, open source components will be provided and further developed on the basis of an open community.  By focusing on reusability, IT costs and IT complexity can be reduced. Especially for personalised, individualised production, an operating system for production thus means a massive reduction in costs. profile_meta_description: Azitek is a deep-tech company with a unique approach to RTLS (Real-Time Location Systems), developing technology specifically optimized for location from end to end. Our solution goes beyond software and hardware alone: the communication protocol itself is designed around the demands of real-time location, enabling levels of performance, scalability, and reliability that are not typically achievable with conventional RTLS technologies.  By combining purpose-built hardware, intelligent software, and a location-optimized communication architecture, we help innovative companies and technology providers track and manage assets, tools, and vehicles with real-time visibility and automation. The result is a robust and scalable system that reduces inefficiencies, minimizes losses, and transforms operational data into faster responses, smarter decisions, and better outcomes.

profile_meta_description: Innok Robotics has been one of the technology and market leaders in highly innovative ‘Autonomous Mobile Robots’ (AMRs) since 2012. These robots can also operate outdoors. From development to programming and assembly, Innok Robotics is a one-stop shop for its customers. The unique selling point of Innok robots is that they are not only able to operate indoors in perfectly prepared halls, but are also capable of ‘multiterrain’ operation. They can also be driven indoors on poor floors and without structural measures on the buildings, as well as outdoors in all weather conditions and over obstacles such as ramps and thresholds. The core products are the autonomous transport robot INDUROS, which is primarily aimed at all companies that transport materials and can be used, for example, to automate transport between halls, and the RAINOS watering robot, which can water park areas and is currently used primarily in cemeteries to water graves autonomously.

profile_meta_description: SINFOSY digital GmbH develops innovative solutions for smart manufacturing and intelligent logistics. The aim is to make production and logistics processes more transparent, efficient, and easier to control digitally.  With SINTRA AIPick, SINFOSY presents a novel technology for millimeter-precise 3D localization of objects in industrial environments. Using a smartphone, an app, and a QR code, components, tools, or transport goods can be precisely localized in space—without the need for complex sensor infrastructure or special hardware. AI-supported image analysis enables a cost-effective, flexible, and quickly integrable solution for modern production environments.  Typical applications include tracking components, locating tools and equipment, and supporting assembly and logistics processes.  With SILVA light, we can digitize any machine in 3 minutes.

TerraTech Holding was founded in 2019 by Torsten Blumenthal, Johannes Angebauer and Johannes Westenhöfer. At an AI start-up meeting in Bad Homburg, the founders quickly realised that there were major gaps between the possibilities of AI technology and its actual use in companies. profile_meta_description: High-precision micro drives since 1961  At maxon, we develop and build electric drives that pack some real power. Our DC motors are leading the industry worldwide. They are used wherever the requirements are high and engineers cannot afford compromises: maxon electric motors drive NASA's Mars rovers. They are installed in insulin pumps and surgical power tools. profile_meta_description: Connecting data from the field with cloud software is the core business of Starwit Technologies. Since its founding in 2022 in Wolfsburg, the Starwit team has developed a wide range of software products. These include cloud applications for analyzing sensor and mobility data, as well as AI-driven analysis of video stream data. As part of an innovation ecosystem consisting of partner companies and universities, the Starwit team drives, on the one hand, the transition from research to practical application and, on the other hand, the integration of classical engineering solutions with computer science. In collaboration with Gerhartl Fahrzeugtechnik, we have developed compact solutions for detecting moving objects. The Mobilitix Sensoring Unit (MSU) can be used in a variety of ways – whether as a sensor for traffic measurement, for tracking in logistics processes, or in use on commercial vehicles – the flexible software architecture of the Starwit Awareness Engine makes it possible.

TECHNOKABEL was established in 1982 by a group of engineers, experienced in cable manufacturing. The companys main activity that time was to give technical assistance to cable producers as well as starting-up and commissioning production lines all over the world for famous cable machinery manufacturers. In 1986 the first cables were produced in the own factory. After privatisation in 1992, TECHNOKABEL became a joint stock company with most of the shares owned by the managers.  The company specialises, in particular, in screened flexible high performance cables for analogue and digital data transmission, applied in computer, control, telecommunication and audio-video systems. The cables can be heat and oil resistant, low temperature and abrasive wear resistant, low smoke, halogen free and flame retardant, steel wire or tape armoured. Low voltage power cables are also developed in recent period of time.

profile_meta_description: The three PMD-X projects showcase the integration of the Platform MaterialDigital (PMD) into industrial data ecosystems.   EDCar connects PMD with Manufacturing-X and Gaia-X (with a focus on Catena-X), enabling structured information exchange throughout the supply chain and promoting the circular economy in the automotive industry.   PMD-X-MAPRO establishes a continuous digital data flow in steel processing, from material simulation to process design, and uses semantic PMD technologies and management shells to develop digital twins of finished products with cross-enterprise, machine-readable material data.   AeroMatPass-X transforms aviation, which is currently document-based, into a material-centric system with sovereign data for digital product passports. The core innovations are a material ontology and the integration of external testing laboratories.

profile_meta_description: IMENSUS is transforming subsurface exploration for mining by delivering fully autonomous, high-resolution geological models as a service. AM-Flow — Automated Quality Inspection for Production Workflows  As quality documentation demands rise and production scales, post-production becomes a bottleneck. Many manufacturers still rely on manual sampling, leading to inconsistent data and processes that don’t scale. In industries like automotive, medical, and aerospace, certified dimensional compliance is now essential.  AM-Flow solves this with a modular automation line covering feeding, identification, inspection, sorting, and packaging—powered by AM-Logic AI and integrated with ERP, MES, and CAD/CAM.  Geometry-based AI identifies any part in 0.2 seconds—no barcodes or RFID needed—across technologies like 3D printing, molding, and CNC.  AM-Quality performs full 3D inspection using 8 laser scanners, comparing each part to its CAD model in real time. Every part gets a report. ISO 10360-13 compliant, at 5–10 seconds per part.  Result: certified, traceable quality at production speed—no added headcount.

profile_meta_description: The Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft, based in Germany, is one of the leading organisations for application-oriented research. The Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft is the leading organization for applied research in Europe. Its research activities are conducted by 72 institutes and research units at locations throughout Germany. The Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft employs a staff of more than 26,600, who work with an annual research budget totaling more than 2.5 billion euros. Of this sum, more than 2.1 billion euros is generated through contract research. Around 70 percent of the Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft’s contract research revenue is derived from contracts with industry and from publicly financed research projects.  International collaborations with excellent research partners and innovative companies around the world ensure direct access to regions of the greatest importance to present and future scientific progress and economic development.

website_about_meta_description: Nuri Launch Vehicle and the Story Behind the Thermal Vacuum ChamberDid you watch the recent launch of the Nuri launch vehicle?Carrying a total of 13 satellites, Nuri successfully lifted off into space.Developed entirely with domestic technology, Nuri is a mid-sized launch vehicle that has successfully carried out satellite deployment missions, marking an important milestone in the growth of Korea’s space technology and the expansion of the private space industry ecosystem.Satellites launched aboard Nuri, however, must undergo extremely rigorous verification processes on the ground before heading into space.This is because even the smallest malfunction cannot be tolerated in the harsh environment of space.Space Equipment Designed to Withstand Extreme EnvironmentsSpace equipment such as satellites, launch vehicles, and space probes must operate reliably under extreme conditions, including high vacuum, high temperatures, and cryogenic temperatures, depending on mission requirements.To verify this performance, a variety of tests that accurately simulate the space environment are conducted on the ground. These include bake-out tests, thermal cycling tests, and thermal vacuum tests.Through these tests, engineers verify whether space equipment and components can maintain stable functionality under extreme conditions. A thermal vacuum chamber is the essential test facility required throughout this process.Thermal Vacuum Chambers That Recreate the Space EnvironmentA thermal vacuum chamber creates a high-vacuum environment inside the chamber and uses a heat exchange system known as a shroud to reproduce the high-temperature and cryogenic conditions experienced by satellites in orbit.This enables space environment testing on the ground, from individual components to full systems, allowing thorough verification of performance and reliability prior to launch.MTES’s Thermal Vacuum Chamber TechnologyMTES is a specialized company capable of designing, manufacturing, and maintaining small-, medium-, and large-scale thermal vacuum chambers.By flexibly applying various design parameters—such as temperature range, vacuum level, chamber size, and materials—MTES provides customized thermal vacuum chamber solutions that reflect real space environment testing requirements, backed by extensive hands-on manufacturing experience.Verification Before Reaching SpaceEvery piece of equipment destined for space must first secure reliability through rigorous ground-based testing before it reaches the launch pad.Thermal vacuum chambers represent the starting point of this verification process, and MTES continues to set the standard for space environment testing through proven expertise and accumulated technical know-how.

3D digital twin allows industrial user to visualize in 3D and real-time all areas of production using our virtual sensor capability, including areas of difficult access and areas without physical sensors, so that the onset of any production problem can be easily determined by virtually zooming to any component of the machine to understand the problem root cause and run a couple of simulations to solve it. planqc's quantum computers store information in individual atoms – nature's best qubits. Quantum information is processed by arranging these qubits in highly scalable arrays and manipulating them with precisely controlled laser pulses. planqc’s unique combination of quantum technologies is the fastest way to scale to thousands of qubits, a prerequisite for industry-relevant quantum advantage. planqc's founding team combines decades of international research on neutral-atom quantum technologies. Our quantum computers are built on the precision of the world’s best atomic clocks, the world’s best quantum gas microscopes, and high-speed Rydberg gates. planqc is the first startup to emerge from the Munich Quantum Valley, which with its vast network of research institutes and industrial partners fosters a unique quantum ecosystem for startups.

SaxonQ GmbH develops and markets mobile quantum computers operating at room temperature. The semiconductor chip-based diamond quantum processor contains NV centers. Our patented NV qubit manufacturing process combines low energy ion implantation with high lateral resolution. This enables the fabrication of large qubit arrays consisting of individual NV centers with nanometer-scale precision and high yield. The connected nuclear qubits provide long coherence times and effective quantum information storage; multi-qubit operations are possible. SaxonQ's unique QC operate on a regular socket and are maintenance free. They offer advantages that enable widespread and effective usage of quantum technologies. A range of applications can be envisioned already today, and quantum algorithms written in Qiskit or openQASM can be executed on our quantum processor. Applications developed include the areas of Medical&Pharma, Mobility, Finance&Marketing, Energy, Aerospace. Get Entangled!™

profile_meta_description: The development of a climate-neutral air transport system is a key component of sustainable mobility in a globalised world. The aim is to significantly increase the energy efficiency of future commercial aircraft through the consistent further development of aircraft and propulsion technologies. The Decide4ECO project focuses on analyzing and assessing sustainability requirements and achieving sustainability goals. DILICO supports you in better understanding, developing, and operating fuel cells, electrolyzers, and batteries reliably. To achieve this, we develop precise measurement and analysis technologies that allow you to transparently monitor the condition and performance of your electrochemical cells and optimize them in a targeted way.  With DILICO Cell Voltage, you can monitor the voltage of individual cells within a stack and detect faults at an early stage. The Cell Voltage Pickup System enables fast and reliable contacting for cell voltage measurements. DILICO CURR TEMP custom measures current and temperature directly inside the stack and provides deeper insight into the real operating behavior of your system. With the Hydrogen Cell Test System, you can analyze electrolyzer cells under reproducible test conditions.  This helps you increase efficiency, extend system lifetime, and improve operational reliability. We also support you with engineering expertise and technical consulting.

profile_meta_description: The German Research Center for Artificial Intelligence (DFKI) was founded in 1988 as a non-profit public-private partnership (PPP). It maintains locations in Kaiserslautern, Saarbrücken, Bremen, Lower Saxony, and Darmstadt, laboratories in Berlin and Lübeck, and a branch office in Trier.  DFKI combines cutting-edge scientific achievement and industry-oriented value creation with societal recognition. For over 35 years, DFKI has been conducting research on AI for the benefit of humanity, focusing on societal relevance and scientific excellence in key, future-oriented research and application areas of artificial intelligence. DFKI is considered one of the most important centers of excellence in the international scientific community.

profile_meta_description: The 8ra Initiative (pronounced: oora, /-O:rə/) is the European umbrella initiative with the goal of creating open, sovereign, and future-proof digital infrastructure and services in Europe. At its core lies the develop-ment of a Multi-Provider Cloud-Edge Continuum – a connected IT architecture that enables flexible, in-teroperable use of cloud and edge resources across providers and national borders. This continuum serves as the foundation for next-generation digital services such as artificial intelligence, autonomous mobility, block-chain and quantum applications.  The 8ra Initiative emerged from the European funding framework IPCEI-CIS (Important Project of Common European Interest – Next Generation Cloud Infrastructure and Services). Today, the initiative brings together around 120 companies from industry, research, and technology.

profile_meta_description: Forschungszentrum Jülich (FZJ) researches and develops key technologies for a digitalized society, the climate-friendly transformation of energy systems, and sustainable economic activity. As one of Europe’s largest interdisciplinary research institutions and home to the first European exascale computer JUPITER, Jülich harnesses expertise in supercomputing, artificial intelligence, quantum computing, hydrogen, battery research, materials science, and neuroscience, as well as emerging fields like the bioeconomy.  Science and industry have access to unique infrastructures including supercomputers, quantum systems, and high-resolution electron microscopes. This accelerates the transfer of scientific insights into technological innovations and industrial applications. At Forschungszentrum Jülich, member of the Helmholtz Association, more than 7,600 employees from over 110 countries work alongside some 1,500 visiting scientists.

company_name: Fraunhofer-Institut für Fertigungstechnik und Angewandte Materialforschung IFAM
profile_meta_description: Fraunhofer IFAM Dresden is a leading research institute in powder metallurgy and conducts application-oriented basic and industrial research for the development of innovative sintered, composite, and functional materials as well as cellular metallic materials for energy, mobility, and medical technology. A key focus in energy technology is hydrogen technology.  The institute accompanies the entire innovation process – from material and technology development to prototype production and transfer to industrial application. Technological focal points include powder-based additive manufacturing processes such as Selective Electron Beam Melting, Additive Screen Printing, Fused Filament Fabrication, GelCasting, MoldJet, and LMM, as well as complementary special technologies such as melt spinning, melt extraction, spark plasma sintering, and molding processes.

Innovations for the digital society of the future are the focus of research and development work at the Fraunhofer Heinrich Hertz Institute HHI. In this area, Fraunhofer HHI is a world leader in the development of mobile and optical communication networks and systems, fiber optical sensor systems as well as the processing and coding of video signals. Together with international partners from research and industry, Fraunhofer HHI works in the whole spectrum of digital infrastructure - from fundamental research to the development of prototypes and solutions. The institute develops standards for information and communication technologies and creates new applications as an industry partner.

profile_meta_description: HCP Sense, a spin-off from TU Darmstadt, offers a simple and economical solution for measuring bearing loads and monitoring lubrication. This information opens up new opportunities for equipment manufacturers and operators, e.g. for digital business models. Load and lubrication data are essential for predictive maintenance and for optimizing manufacturing processes. In discussions with equipment manufacturers and plant operators, we have recognized that the effort required for adaptation designs is a major obstacle to the integration of sensors. For this reason, we have developed a patented measuring method that turns conventional bearings into sensors and requires only minimal design adjustments. For the user, it is therefore very easy and economical to integrate our sensor technology into existing designs.  We would be happy to advise you on the beneficial options for using our sensor bearing in your applications. For plain and roller bearings!

profile_meta_description: Since 1996, the OPC Foundation has facilitated the development and adoption of the OPC information exchange standards. As both advocate and custodian of these specifications, the Foundation’s mission is to help industry vendors, end-users, and software developers maintain interoperability in their manufacturing and automation assets. The OPC Foundation is dedicated to providing the best specifications, technology, process, and certification to achieve multivendor, multiplatform, secure, reliable, interoperability for moving data and information from the embedded world to the enterprise cloud. The Foundation serves over 920 members worldwide in the Industrial Automation, IT, IoT, IIoT, M2M, Industrie 4.0, Building Automation, machine tools, pharmaceutical, petrochemical, and Smart Energy sectors.

company_name: Universität Saarland iMSL - Intelligent Material Systems
profile_meta_description: The Chair of Intelligent Material Systems (iMSL) at Saarland University conducts research on the development of mechatronic actuator and sensor systems based on intelligent materials. The focus lies on holistic system solutions-from fundamental research to industrial application-as well as on the development of intelligent, application-specific electronics for these technologies. The developed systems are characterized by high flexibility, fast actuation, and minimal space requirements. iMSL is currently transitioning into the Center for Intelligent Material Systems (CiMS), Saarland Center dedicated to the industrialization of intelligent material systems. Its goal is to develop automated manufacturing processes for dielectric elastomer actuators as well as joining technologies for shape memory alloy (SMA) actuators. In collaboration with mateligent GmbH, these innovative technologies are transferred into series production and brought to market.

The goal of the AutomationML association is the dissemination and further development of the AutomationML data format as an open, neutral and free industrial standard for the cross-company and interdisciplinary exchange of planning data for plants in the manufacturing and process industry. For this purpose, no new data format has been developed, but rather existing formats have been used and suitably extended, adapted and merged. In this way, it has so far been possible to map plant data, starting with the plant topology, through geometry and kinematics information, right up to processes and logical dependencies. AutomationML is currently the most comprehensive data format for plant engineering, is available in the first products, is already being used industrially and is being upgraded for further data volumes. It is internationally standardized as an open, free and XML-based standard within IEC 62714.

profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Hedro Smart Sensors
profile_meta_description: Hedro develops wireless sensors and edge computing solutions for real-time monitoring of legacy industrial machines. Our technology enables condition and process monitoring without replacing existing equipment, accelerating the transition to Industry 4.0.  Our sensors operate in near real time, measuring data every second while maintaining a viable battery lifetime. This enables continuous process monitoring and machinery protection beyond periodic condition checks. High-resolution vibration, temperature, and operational data are processed at the edge, enabling fast responses, predictive maintenance, and improved process stability.  Hedro’s solutions can be used directly by end users or integrated by partners as a white-label solution, allowing OEMs and integrators to add wireless real-time vibration monitoring to their portfolios. Designed for harsh industrial environments, our systems integrate seamlessly with existing automation and IT infrastructures.

profile_meta_description: In the PoQuaSIA project, Rheinmetall and MIRA are developing a digital ecosystem that includes a generic control platform for the teleoperation of vehicles—as a building block for new mobility concepts for vehicles on the road, water, or rail. The performance of the overall system, consisting of a highly secure cloud infrastructure, IoT platform, security modules, and real-time fleet and route management, has been successfully demonstrated with vehicles in road traffic and intralogistics, agricultural machinery, and robotics. AI-supported applications for recording and processing large amounts of data in real time and for increasing network availability have been developed and tested. Post-quantum secure encryption methods are used to ensure a high level of security during data exchange.

profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Industrial Digital Twin Association
profile_meta_description: The Industrial Digital Twin Association (IDTA) is the international organisation for the standardisation of the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) – the digital twin for industrial assets.  The AAS enables a standardised, machine-readable representation of products, machines and plants – providing the technological basis for the EU Digital Product Passport and industrial ecosystems such as Manufacturing-X. IDTA develops specifications, Submodel Templates and implementation guidelines, coordinates international standardisation (IEC 63278) and drives global AAS adoption. The goal: digital interoperability as the foundation for resilient, sustainable and competitive industrial value creation.

GREEN-TWIN is part of the 8ra initiative and a project by N+P Informationssysteme aimed at developing a sustainable, hybrid platform for digital twins in the context of green buildings and factories. The starting point is the situation faced by many SMEs: they want to make use of real-time data from the operation of their machines and systems in customer environments (e.g. for monitoring, optimization, and new digital services), but at the same time, there are often reservations about handing over data entirely to a single cloud provider. To address this, GREEN-TWIN relies on a sovereign cloud solution with hybrid platform environments and data spaces such as Manufacturing-X for multi-provider infrastructure scenarios. The aim is to enable trusted, cross-company data usage. To support interoperability, data sovereignty, and data control, the project integrates GAIA-X core components and standards.

According to the principle "bringing-in, going global", CIPA works in line with China's economic strategies and cooperates with international business organizations, foreign investment promotion agencies, chambers of commerce and business associations on behalf of the PRC Ministry of Commerce.

company_name: Star Titanium New Materials Co., Ltd.
profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Star Titanium New Materials
profile_meta_description: Shaanxi Star Titanium is a national high-tech enterprise specializing in the research and development, production, and sale of high-quality titanium alloy wires and profiles. Guided by the core concept of “Titanium Innovation, Smart Future Manufacturing,” the company relies on the leading R&D teams at the Harbin Institute of Technology, Northwestern Polytechnical University, and the Aerospace and Military Industry Institute to vigorously promote the research, development, and industrial application of titanium alloy technology. It is committed to overcoming the military industry’s reliance on imported key materials and advancing the upgrading of titanium technology in the civilian sector.
